# Edgecut & Co. Design System — Google Labs DESIGN.md v0.1.1
# Generated by design-md-generator (salon industry) for multi-tenant barbershop marketplace

version: alpha
name: Edgecut & Co.
description: "Sharp lines. Honest cuts. Walk in, walk out fresh. — Design system for a 3-tenant barbershop marketplace (Brooklyn, Los Angeles, Madrid) with marketplace token extensions for provider search, booking, and business operations."

  on-success: "#FFFFFF"

  warning-surface: "#FEF1DD"

  border: "#D9CFC4"
  border-light: "#E8E0D6"

  tenant-madrid-accent: "#8A1A3E"

# Tenant-specific component overrides
# These are applied via [data-tenant="brooklyn|la|madrid"] CSS selectors
tenant-brooklyn:
  colors:
    accent: "{colors.tenant-brooklyn-accent}"
tenant-la:
  colors:
    accent: "{colors.tenant-la-accent}"
tenant-madrid:
  colors:
    accent: "{colors.tenant-madrid-accent}"

---

## Overview

Edgecut & Co. is a multi-location barbershop brand with three distinct locations — Brooklyn (Bedford-Stuy), Los Angeles (Silver Lake), and Madrid (Malasaña). The design system serves a marketplace platform connecting clients with independent barbers across three tenants, each with its own currency, locale, and timezone.

The brand persona is clean, confident, and unpretentious. The visual language balances warm analog textures (leather, brass, tile) with crisp digital precision. The core audience is 22–45 year-old men, women, and non-binary clients who value craftsmanship and convenience equally.

**Design principles:**
- **Sharp & Honest** — No decorative fluff. Every element has purpose. Borders are crisp, contrast is confident.
- **Warm Industrial** — Deep charcoals and warm taupes with brass/gold accents. Feels like a well-kept shop, not a sterile app.
- **Locally Rooted** — Each tenant gets a subtle accent color drawn from its neighborhood character (Brooklyn blue, LA terra cotta, Madrid crimson).
- **Mobile-First** — 60% mobile traffic drives the layout hierarchy. Touch targets are ≥44px. Bottom navigation on customer app.
- **Marketplace Honesty** — Realtime indicators show true freshness. Deposit amounts are explicit. Cancel policies are unavoidable.

## Colors

### Core palette

The brand is grounded in a near-black (`#1A1C1E`) that reads as charcoal, not dead black. The secondary is a warm brass/gold (`#B8926B`) that catches the eye without screaming. The surface is a warm off-white (`#FAF7F4`) — like aged paper or a barber's apron.

**Primary** (`#1A1C1E`) — Used for text, primary buttons, and structural elements. High authority. Not quite black.

**Secondary** (`#B8926B`) — Accents, badges, hover states, decorative lines. Warm, approachable.

**Surface** (`#FAF7F4`) — Page backgrounds, card surfaces. Warm enough to feel tactile, neutral enough for text readability.

### Marketplace token extensions

Per Marketplace Rule 16, the following tokens are defined independently of the accent palette:

| Token | Usage | Color |
|---|---|---|
| `--rating-color` | Star ratings (filled) | `#E8A838` |
| `--distance-color` | Distance display text | `#5A7D8C` |
| `--available-now-bg` | "Available now" badge bg | `#E8F5EE` |
| `--available-now-fg` | "Available now" badge text | `#2A7D4F` |
| `--verified-bg` | Verified review badge bg | `#E0EEF8` |
| `--verified-fg` | Verified review badge text | `#1A6F9E` |
| `--deposit-pill-bg` | Deposit amount pill bg | `#FEF1DD` |
| `--deposit-pill-fg` | Deposit amount pill text | `#8A5E1A` |
| `--policy-warn-bg` | Policy/cancel warning bg | `#FBEAEA` |
| `--policy-warn-fg` | Policy/cancel warning text | `#C13535` |
| `--sponsored-bg` | Sponsored label background | `#F0EAE4` |
| `--sponsored-fg` | Sponsored label text | `#6B6258` |

None of these reuse the `--accent` or `--secondary` tokens. They are independently tunable for contrast and brand fit.

### Contrast ratios (WCAG AA)

All primary text on surface pairs exceed 4.5:1. The lowest contrast pair is `--on-surface-dim` on `--surface-dim` at 4.8:1. Error/success/warning surfaces meet 4.5:1 on their respective text values.

### High-contrast override

When `prefers-contrast: more` is active, the palette shifts to stricter contrast:
- Surface shifts from `#FAF7F4` to `#FFFFFF`
- Primary shifts from `#1A1C1E` to `#000000`
- Secondary shifts from `#B8926B` to `#8B6F4E`
- Border shifts from `#D9CFC4` to `#6B6258`
- All marketplace tokens check 4.5:1 minimum; the `--deposit-pill-bg` shifts to `#FDF0CC` for better fg contrast.

## Typography

### Typeface pairing

| Role | Typeface | Fallback | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Display** | DM Serif Display | Georgia, serif | H1s, hero headlines, section titles |
| **UI** | Inter | system-ui, sans-serif | Body, buttons, labels, navigation |
| **Mono** | JetBrains Mono | Menlo, monospace | Code, numbers, metadata |

DM Serif Display brings a editorial, craft-oriented feel to headlines — like a magazine spread about the art of barbering. Inter keeps the UI crisp and legible at every size. The combination says "we take the craft seriously."

### Scale

The type scale follows a 1.25 modular scale (major third) for display sizes and a 1.125 scale (major second) for body sizes. Line heights decrease for larger sizes (tighter = more impact) and increase for body sizes (looser = more readable at length).

## Layout

### Grid system

12-column grid on screens ≥1024px, 6 columns on 640–1023px, full-bleed single column below 640px. Gutters are 24px (desktop) / 16px (tablet) / 16px (mobile).

### Breakpoints

| Name | Width | Columns |
|---|---|---|
| Mobile | <640px | 1 (full bleed) |
| Tablet | 640–1023px | 6 |
| Desktop | 1024–1439px | 12 |
| Wide | ≥1440px | 12 (max 1280px content) |

### Max content width

1280px for page content. Sections can be full-bleed with inner content constrained.

### Provider grid (marketplace-specific)

The provider search grid uses a responsive card layout:
- Mobile: 1 column
- Tablet: 2 columns
- Desktop: 3 columns
- Cards are equal-height via flexbox. Filter bar remains visible above the grid.

### Spacing

Spacing follows a 4px base grid. The standard rhythm uses 8/16/24/32/48/64/96 increments. Section spacing is 96px (desktop) / 64px (tablet) / 48px (mobile).

## Elevation & Depth

Edgecut & Co. uses a flat aesthetic with subtle elevation cues. Shadows are avoided in favor of thin borders and background-color shifts to indicate hierarchy.

| Layer | Elevation | Treatment |
|---|---|---|
| Surface | 0 | `background: var(--surface)` |
| Raised (cards, modals) | +1 | Subtle `box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(26,28,30,0.08)` |
| Overlay (dropdowns, pickers) | +2 | `box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(26,28,30,0.12)` |
| Dialog (modals, sheets) | +3 | `box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(26,28,30,0.16)` + backdrop blur 4px |

Elevation is always achieved via box-shadow + background, never via `z-index` abuse. The backdrop overlay is `rgba(26,28,30,0.4)` with `backdrop-filter: blur(2px)`.

## Shapes

### Border radius

| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `--rounded-sm` | 4px | Badges, small buttons, input fields |
| `--rounded-md` | 8px | Standard buttons, cards, dialogs |
| `--rounded-lg` | 12px | Large cards, sheets, containers |
| `--rounded-xl` | 16px | Modal dialogs, profile images |
| `--rounded-full` | 9999px | Pills, avatars, realtime dots |

Cards and buttons use `rounded-md` (8px) by default. This feels modern without being aggressively rounded. The deposit-pill and verified-badge use `rounded-full` for a capsule shape that distinguishes them from standard UI elements.

### Borders

Standard border is 1px solid `--border` (`#D9CFC4`). Active/focused states use 2px solid `--primary` with a 1px offset. Error borders use 2px solid `--error`.

## Components

### Button – Primary
Full-width or inline. Dark background, white text. Used for primary CTAs (Book Now, Confirm, Continue). Hover lightens background. Active darkens. Disabled goes grey.

### Button – Secondary
Ghost/outline style. Transparent background with text color. Used for secondary actions (Cancel, Back, View Profile). Hover gets surface-alt background.

### Card
Warm off-white background with subtle shadow. Used for barber profiles, service cards, booking summaries. Hover state shifts to surface-alt for interactive cards.

### Input Field
Standard text input with label and optional helper text. Focus state uses 2px primary border. Validation error adds error border + error-surface background.

### Nav Item
Navigation link with hover and active states. Active state uses a thicker bottom border or filled background. Sidebar nav uses pill-shaped active indicators.

### Deposit Pill
Small capsule badge showing the deposit amount with explicit "hold" label (e.g., "$25 hold"). Background is `--deposit-pill-bg`, text uses `--deposit-pill-fg`. Always paired with total amount nearby so the user can distinguish hold from total.

### Verified Badge
Small badge on reviews marked "Verified purchase." Background is `--verified-bg`, text uses `--verified-fg`. Icon optional.

### Realtime Dot
8px circle indicator. Three states:
- `--fresh-dot` (green): data updated <30s ago
- `--stale-dot` (orange): data updated 30–60s ago, shows "Stale · Xs ago" label
- `--dead-dot` (grey): data updated >60s ago, shows "Last updated Xm ago"

### Stale Badge
Inline badge that appears when realtime data is stale. Shows time since last update. Appears next to the relevant data element (available-now dots, slot grid timestamps).

### Cancel Policy Banner
Full-width banner on barber profile and checkout step showing the cancellation policy. Warning-styled (light red bg, red text). Includes the exact policy text; must match biz-policies.html SSoT.

## Do's and Don'ts

- **DO** use `--deposit-pill-bg` for deposit badges. Don't reuse `--secondary` — deposit info is distinct from decorative accent.
- **DO** show the cancel policy above the Book CTA on every barber profile and as a required checkbox at checkout.
- **DO** use realtime dot semantics honestly. Green = <30s. Never show green on stale data.
- **DO** show provider timezone explicitly ("Times shown in PT — barber is in Los Angeles"). Never silently convert to client TZ.
- **DON'T** use `--accent` for rating stars. Use `--rating-color`.
- **DON'T** show a green realtime dot on data older than 30s. Use orange or grey with time label.
- **DON'T** duplicate cancel/deposit policy text. Load from `biz-policies.html` (the single source of truth).
- **DON'T** use `border`/`outline`/`elevation` as component sub-tokens in CSS variable names. These belong in CSS rule declarations, not DESIGN.md tokens.
- **DON'T** show sponsored/featured barber cards without a visible, non-hover "Sponsored" label (≥10px, ≥3:1 contrast).
- **DO** include `padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` on mobile bottom nav for iOS home indicator compliance.
